result:
Done. The spec is fully implemented in `CsvRow.cs`, the project builds cleanly with zero warnings, and a runtime sanity check confirms the behavior.

What I added to `CsvRow.cs` (the only editable source file):

- **`EscapeField(string)`** — returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it doubles internal quotes and wraps the result in double-quotes.
- **`JoinRow(List<string>)`** — escapes each field and joins with commas; the empty list yields the empty string.
- **`SplitRow(string)`** — a single-pass parser: a field starting with `"` is scanned to its closing un-doubled quote (with `""` contributing one literal quote, and commas/CR/LF inside treated as content); otherwise the field runs to the next comma or end of line. The empty line parses to one empty field, and a trailing comma produces a trailing empty field.

I verified with a throwaway console app in a temp directory (outside the project, so no test files were added): escaping edge cases (`empty`, comma, embedded quotes, CR/LF) and `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields)) == fields` round-trips for several tricky non-empty field lists — including fields that are just `"`, `""`, `,`, and mixes — all passed. Used 1 of the 10 build cycles for the library itself.
